name: CIMA ZM03: Laguna de los Peces Cycling Route
name_original: Rodeando el Lago de Sanabria — Circular en la Comarca de Sanabria
description: Cycle the moderate 15.4-mile Laguna de los Peces route in Sanabria, offering spectacular views over Lake Sanabria and surrounding nature.
region_intro: Touring cycling around Galende, located within the Sanabria Lake Natural Park in Zamora, Spain, offers diverse terrain for cyclists. The region is characterized by Sanabria Lake, the largest glacial lake on the Iberian Peninsula, and the Tera River, which flows through the area. The landscape includes mountains, other glacial lagoons, and extensive forests primarily composed of oak, holly, yew, birch, and alder trees. High peaks such as Peña Trevinca contribute to the dramatic scenery, while many routes feature a mix of paved surfaces and traditional dirt roads suitable for touring. Best touring cycling routes around Galende The most popular touring cycling route is Puebla-Lago de Sanabria MTB Route, a 31.6 miles (50.9 km) trail that takes 3 hours 32 minutes to complete. This route winds through the Lago de Sanabria Natural Park, offering views of the lake and Tera River, with sections featuring mostly paved surfaces. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is CIMA ZM03: Laguna de los Peces Cycling Route, a moderate 15.4 miles (24.8 km) path. It provides spectacular views of Lake Sanabria and its natural surroundings, with significant portions considered very easy. Local touring cyclists also love the Sanabria Villages Loop, a 15.8 miles (25.4 km) trail leading through varied terrain and local villages, often completed in about 1 hour 54 minutes. Touring cycling around Galende is defined by glacial lakes, river valleys, and extensive oak forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more difficult routes with significant elevation gains. The routes in Galende are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 7 reviews. More than 120 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Galende's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes around Galende?
A: The terrain around Galende is quite varied, offering a mix of landscapes. You'll find routes with mostly paved surfaces, as well as traditional paths and dirt roads. The region is characterized by the large glacial Sanabria Lake, the Tera River, mountains, other glacial lagoons, and extensive oak forests. This diversity ensures options for various cycling styles and preferences.

Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in the Galende area?
A: There are over 25 touring cycling routes documented around Galende. These routes cater to different skill levels, with 2 easy, 8 moderate, and 15 difficult options, ensuring there's something for every touring cyclist.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Galende?
A: Yes, Galende offers several circular touring cycling routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1402625659/sanabria-villages-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanabria Villages Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 25.4 km trail leading through varied terrain and local villages. Another challenging circular route is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1036887181/rio-de-onor-from-puebla-de-sanabria-circular-via-pedralba-de-la-praderia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rio de Onor from Puebla de Sanabria – circular via Pedralba de la Praderia&lt;/a&gt;, covering about 40 km.

Q: What are some scenic viewpoints or natural features I can see while cycling in Galende?
A: Galende is rich in natural beauty. Along your rides, you can encounter stunning sights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5423435" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de los Peces&lt;/a&gt;, the dramatic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4378888" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Trevinca Summit&lt;/a&gt;, and various viewpoints offering panoramic vistas of Sanabria Lake, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5423413" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint of Lake Sanabria&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4708534" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sotillo Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; is another beautiful natural monument worth visiting.

Q: Are there any routes suitable for beginners or families with children?
A: While many routes in Galende have moderate to difficult ratings, some sections are considered very easy. For instance, significant portions of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1402553550/cima-zm03-laguna-de-los-peces-cycling-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;CIMA ZM03: Laguna de los Peces Cycling Route&lt;/a&gt; are accessible and enjoyable for touring cyclists, making it a good option for those seeking a less strenuous ride with spectacular views.

Q: What historical or cultural attractions can I explore near the cycling routes?
A: Beyond natural landscapes, Galende and its surroundings offer historical and cultural points of interest. You can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/910086" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of the Counts of Benavente&lt;/a&gt; or explore the charming &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3834068" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Town of Puebla de Sanabria&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3834076" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parish of Saint James the Apostle&lt;/a&gt; is another notable religious building in the area.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about cycling in Galende?
A: The touring cycling routes in Galende are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 7 reviews. More than 120 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Galende's varied terrain, often praising the stunning views of Sanabria Lake and the diverse landscapes.

Q: Is it possible to combine cycling with other activities like wild swimming?
A: Given the prominence of Sanabria Lake and its surrounding natural park, there are opportunities to combine cycling with other outdoor activities. The lake is the largest glacial lake on the Iberian Peninsula, and you can find spots like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5414757" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Lake Sanabria from Arenales Beach&lt;/a&gt;, which could be suitable for a refreshing stop during your ride.

Q: Are there any challenging routes for experienced touring cyclists?
A: Yes, Galende offers several challenging routes for experienced touring cyclists. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1036915640/puebla-lago-de-sanabria-mtb-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puebla-Lago de Sanabria MTB Route&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 50.9 km trail with significant elevation changes. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1404123450/laguna-de-los-peces-cima-zm03-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de los Peces CIMA ZM03 Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a 36.6 km route also rated as difficult.

Q: What kind of natural environment defines the touring cycling experience in Galende?
A: The touring cycling experience in Galende is defined by its unique natural environment within the Sanabria Lake Natural Park. You'll cycle through areas featuring glacial lakes, river valleys carved by the Tera River, and extensive forests primarily composed of oak, holly, yew, birch, and alder trees. High peaks like Peña Trevinca also contribute to the dramatic scenery.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Galende?
A: While specific seasonal data isn't provided, the region's diverse landscapes, including mountains and glacial lakes, suggest that spring and autumn would likely offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery for touring cycling. Summers can be warm, and winters might bring colder conditions, especially at higher elevations.
tips: A small town in the municipality of Galende, Zamora near the border with Ourense, Galicia. Ribadelago, located near the Sanabria Lake Natural Park, has a tragic past. In 1959, the town was devastated by eight million cubic meters of water from the Vega de Tera reservoir.

The waters of the Tera river creating one of the largest natural lakes in Spain, the Sanabria Lake Natural Park. Located in the municipality of Galende, it is a beautiful place to cycle through the Sanabria Lake and Sierras Segundera Natural Park.
Last town where you can get water, it has a convent and the water from the fountain is cold

The view of Lake Sanabria from the Neveira Viewpoint is one of the most spectacular in the natural park. This viewpoint is located near the Monastery of San Martín de Castañeda, on a route that combines history, nature, and breathtaking panoramas.
Nice beach bar on the edge of the lake. It has food service and has toilets. A good place to stop and enjoy the views.
At this point there are wonderful views of the flow of the Tera river before it merges with the Sanabria lake. An extraordinary lake of glacial origin that is the largest in Spain.

faqs.question: What kind of bike is best suited for the Laguna de los Peces Cycling Route?
faqs.answer: While listed as a touring bicycle route, the Laguna de los Peces Cycling Route is primarily recommended as a mountain bike route. It features sections with rough and bumpy asphalt, as well as challenging segments, particularly when crossing the village of San Martín de Castañeda. Touring cyclists with robust bikes and a preference for off-road adventures might enjoy it, but those with road-oriented touring setups may find the terrain demanding.

faqs.question: Is the 'CIMA ZM03' designation relevant to this cycling route?
faqs.answer: No, the 'CIMA ZM03' designation is not related to this cycling route. 'CIMA' refers to the Chartered Institute of Management Accountants, a professional body unrelated to outdoor trails. The route is correctly known as the Laguna de los Peces Cycling Route.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this route?
faqs.answer: The route is mostly easy, but it does include two notably harder sections: one when crossing the village of San Martín de Castañeda, and another at the exit of the second part of the village. The final 2 kilometers can also be challenging due to headwinds. Some paths in the area have very rough and bumpy asphalt, while others are paved.

faqs.question: What are the key points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter several beautiful spots. Highlights include the charming village of Ribadelago, stunning views of Lake Sanabria, and the historic Monastery of San Martín de Castañeda. Don't miss the panoramic View of Lake Sanabria from Mirador de Neveira and a chance to relax at a Chiringuito at Lake Sanabria. You'll also get a great View of the Tera River.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Sanabria region is known for its natural beauty, making spring and autumn ideal for cycling with pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer can also be enjoyable, but be prepared for potentially warmer weather. Winter cycling is possible but may involve colder temperatures and potentially snow or ice, especially at higher elevations.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for cycling in Parque Natural del Lago de Sanabria y Sierras Segundera y de Porto?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for cycling on public trails within the Parque Natural del Lago de Sanabria y Sierras Segundera y de Porto. However, it's always a good idea to check the official park regulations before your visit, as rules can change or specific areas might have restrictions.

faqs.question: Is the Laguna de los Peces Cycling Route suitable for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: While much of the route is described as 'very easy,' there are two notably harder sections and the final 2 kilometers can have headwinds. For families or beginners, it's important to consider these challenging segments. Some general cycling routes around Lake Sanabria are easy and mostly paved, suitable for all fitness levels, but this specific route has more demanding parts that might require good fitness or even pushing your bike.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the route?
faqs.answer: Specific parking information for the Laguna de los Peces Cycling Route is not detailed. However, given its location near Lake Sanabria and the village of San Martín de Castañeda, you can typically find parking in or near these settlements. It's advisable to look for designated parking areas in villages or near popular viewpoints.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several other trails. You'll find yourself on parts of the ~ ZA-103 (40%), ~ Atajo Castañeda (39%), Camino Playa del Folgoso - San Martín (13%), and A Ribadelago (6%). It also briefly touches on Senda de los Monjes (5%) and Sendero de Bouzas (5%), among others.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Laguna de los Peces Cycling Route?
faqs.answer: Information specifically regarding dogs on this route is not provided. As the route is within a Natural Park, general park regulations for pets usually apply. It's common for dogs to be allowed on trails, but often they must be kept on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other visitors. Please check local signage or the park's official website for the most current pet policies.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or drink near the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, you'll find options for refreshments. There's a Chiringuito at Lake Sanabria, which is a great spot to stop. Additionally, the villages around Lake Sanabria, such as Ribadelago and San Martín de Castañeda, typically offer cafes, restaurants, and pubs where you can refuel.
